---
title: "Renewable energy share over two decades"
engine: markdown
date: "2026-05-26"
image: plot-light.svg
categories:
  - 2026
description: "Renewable energy as a share of final consumption, 1990–2010, for five large economies."
---

The Sustainable Energy for All dataset tracks each country's energy mix from 1990 to 2010.
Plotting renewable energy as a share of final consumption shows how differently five large economies started and moved.
Brazil stays high on hydro and biomass, China's share falls as coal grows, and France and Germany climb slowly from a low base.

```{typst}
//| label: fig-2026-05-26
//| file: _plot.typ
//| output-filename: plot.svg
//| echo: true
//| fig-cap: "Renewable energy as a share of total final energy consumption, 1990–2010."
//| fig-alt: "Line chart of renewable energy share for Brazil, China, France, Germany, and the United States from 1990 to 2010."
```

Data: [TidyTuesday 2026-05-26](https://github.com/rfordatascience/tidytuesday/tree/main/data/2026/2026-05-26).
